behaviors.description: An image containing explicit content is flagged and held for review, not published.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is model judgment quality plus enforcement action. A stubbed classifier would hide real misclassification of explicit images, so Integration/System alone is insufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Submit known explicit-image samples through the real vision model path and assert each is marked flagged and routed to hold-for-review (never published).

behaviors.description: A text post containing hate speech is flagged regardless of obfuscation techniques (e.g., letter substitution, unicode lookalikes).
behaviors.justification: This behavior targets robustness of model semantic detection under adversarial text transformations; deterministic mocks would always pass and miss real failures.
behaviors.test_description: Run a corpus of obfuscated hate-speech variants through the real text model path and assert they are flagged and held.

behaviors.description: A post that passes both content checks is published rather than held or silently dropped.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure is cross-module state handoff/decision logic after both checks pass (classifier outputs -> decision -> publish). This can be caught by wiring moderation service, decision engine, and publish path with deterministic passing classifier fakes.
behaviors.test_description: Wire moderation modules end-to-end in-process with both classifiers returning PASS; assert post reaches published state and is not held/dropped.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Rejected Agentic because behavior text is conditional: "passes both content checks". It validates orchestration after PASS, not model-output quality.

behaviors.description: The full moderation pipeline — image check, text check, publish decision — completes within 5 seconds of submission.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end SLA across multiple AI-backed steps in sequence. Real latency interaction across image model call, text model call, and final decision path is the failure boundary; mocks can hide timing spikes and queue effects.
behaviors.test_description: Execute full submission flow with real model calls enabled (image + text) and measure end-to-end completion time from submit to terminal decision; assert p95 (or defined acceptance metric) stays <= 5s under representative load.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Rejected System-only because behavior text explicitly requires the "full moderation pipeline — image check, text check, publish decision" (multiple agentic steps), which meets Workflow criteria.

behaviors.description: When the AI vision model is unavailable, image posts are queued for retry rather than auto-approved.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is failure handling/orchestration for dependency outage (error -> retry queue) and does not require model output quality. An in-process failing vision-client stub can reproduce unavailable responses and catch auto-approve regressions.
behaviors.test_description: Run moderation service with vision adapter forced to return unavailable/timeout errors; assert image posts are persisted to retry queue and never auto-published.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Rejected System because behavior text only states "model is unavailable" and does not require validating real network/OS failure semantics to catch the decision bug.

behaviors.description: The moderation decision for a borderline image is consistent when the same image is submitted multiple times within a short window.
behaviors.justification: Consistency on borderline content depends on real model behavior (and any stochasticity/calibration effects). Mocked constant outputs would mask genuine inconsistency failures.
behaviors.test_description: Submit the same borderline image repeatedly through the real vision-model moderation path within the target window; assert decision consistency meets the defined threshold.
